what_it_does: Acceptance test for the cognition↔resolution loop. It seeds prior and in-flight swarm cognition turns by writing run://<turn>/<role> refs and emitting cognition.role.ran / cognition.turn.done lifecycle events, then asserts that only completed prior turns are resolved into the next turn's chat context via the R2 cognition stratum. It also checks that the loop does not leak into address-attached reads, is suppressed in off mode, respects a bounded turn window and per-role character cap, and skips pruned refs without crashing.
observations: Uses a tempfile.TemporaryDirectory to create an isolated FsStore per run.
Bootstraps a Suite with FsStore and NodeRegistry discovered from the nodes directory.
Creates a real constant node so suite state() works.
Seeds completed turns with run:// refs, cognition.role.ran events (ok=True, address=...), and a cognition.turn.done event.
Seeds in-flight turns with refs and role.ran events but deliberately omits turn.done.
Tests the plain-chat case by asserting current_locus is empty before calling _chat_context.
Looks for hardcoded framing strings such as 'YOUR OWN PRIOR THINKING' and truncation marker '…'.
Mutates suite.R2_COGNITION_TURNS and suite.R2_COGNITION_PER_ROLE to test bounding behavior.
Toggles suite.set_mode between 'off' and 'listening' to test suppression.
Uses unique marker strings (ZEBRA..., OCTOPUS..., AAAA..., etc.) to detect inclusion/exclusion.
standouts: The test is structured as six numbered 'PROOFS' of advisor-flagged traps rather than ordinary unit tests.
It explicitly simulates swarm lifecycle events instead of driving the swarm through its public API.
It frames the desired behavior as 'the system's OWN thinking' feeding back into the next turn.
conventions: Top-of-file docstring contains a detailed design rationale and numbered proof claims.
A global PASS counter and custom check() helper print ok/XX and raise SystemExit on failure.
sys.path is mutated to import from the parent directory.
Tests mix assert statements with the custom check() helper.
concerns: Heavy reliance on private/underscore Suite methods and attributes makes the test brittle to internal refactoring.
Test state is constructed by directly emitting events and writing refs, bypassing public swarm behavior.
Hardcoded output framing strings ('YOUR OWN PRIOR THINKING', '…') create brittle assertions.
Suite mode and cap constants are mutated mid-test without guaranteed restoration on failure.
Mixing assert with check() gives inconsistent failure reporting.
notes: An acceptance test that wires the swarm's own prior-turn run outputs into the next turn's R2 chat context and guards against in-flight leakage, unbounded flooding, and silent address-read contamination.
questions: What is the public API contract for _chat_context, _r2_cognition_strata, and _r2_gather?
How do Suite.set_mode values 'off' and 'listening' affect stratum resolution exactly?
What component normally emits cognition.role.ran and cognition.turn.done events in production?
How does the R2 resolver distinguish the cognition stratum from other strata?
Is the truncation marker '…' locale-dependent or configurable?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it proves the J1 cognition↔resolution loop behavior through seeded turns and explicit assertions.
intention_signals: Make the swarm's own prior-turn thinking resolvable by the next turn's R2 resolution without introducing a second resolver or index, while preventing leaks into generic address reads and avoiding unbounded context growth.
novelty: Treats acceptance testing as explicit numbered 'proofs' of advisor-flagged traps, using synthetic lifecycle-event emission to simulate swarm cognition stages.
contribution: A concrete set of acceptance assertions that encode the expected behavior of the cognition↔resolution loop, including the turn-done gate, bounded truncation, off-mode suppression, and graceful handling of missing refs.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the cognition↔resolution loop that seeds completed and in-flight swarm turns and verifies prior cognition is resolved into the next turn's chat context only when bounded, opted-in, and not leaking into address-attached reads.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the swarm's own prior-turn outputs feed the next turn's R2 resolution while excluding in-flight turns, capping length, suppressing in off mode, and skipping pruned refs.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that prints ok/XX, counts passes, and exits on failure.
symbols.description: The check function takes a label and a boolean condition, prints a pass/fail marker, increments the global PASS counter when true, and raises SystemExit with the label when false. It is the primary assertion driver for the acceptance checks.

symbols.does: Constructs a Suite instance over a temporary FsStore with discovered nodes.
symbols.description: The _suite helper creates an FsStore from the provided root path, discovers nodes via NodeRegistry from the project's nodes directory, and returns a configured Suite object used by the acceptance test.

symbols.does: Seeds a completed swarm cognition turn with run refs and lifecycle events.
symbols.description: For each role in role_outputs, this helper writes a run://<turn>/<role> content ref, emits a cognition.role.ran event with ok=True and the address, and finally emits cognition.turn.done so the turn is treated as completed for the resolution loop.

symbols.does: Seeds an in-flight swarm cognition turn that lacks the turn-done event.
symbols.description: This helper writes run://<turn>/<role> refs and emits cognition.role.ran events for each role, but deliberately does not emit cognition.turn.done, leaving the turn in-flight so tests can verify the turn-done gate excludes it from context resolution.
